task_completion.description: Three audio tracks named 'Guide Track', 'ADR Record', and 'Beep Track' are created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The track headers on the left side of the Ardour editor window display the names 'Guide Track', 'ADR Record', and 'Beep Track'.

task_completion.description: The narration audio is imported onto the 'Guide Track' at 0:00 and the track is muted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An audio region (block with a waveform) is visible on the 'Guide Track' starting exactly at the beginning of the timeline (0:00). The 'M' (Mute) button on the 'Guide Track' header is highlighted or active.
The record enable button (typically a red circle) on the 'ADR Record' track header is highlighted or active.

task_completion.description: Exactly three distinct audio regions are present on the 'Beep Track' to serve as a countdown cue.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Exactly three separate, distinct audio regions (blocks with waveforms) are visible on the 'Beep Track' timeline, typically placed before the 5.0-second mark.

task_completion.description: The session's loop range is set from 5.0 seconds to 15.0 seconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A loop marker (usually a green bar or bracketed region in the ruler/timeline area at the top) starts exactly at the 5.0-second mark and ends exactly at the 15.0-second mark.

integrity.description: The agent used the Ardour GUI to configure the session.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Ardour application window open with active interactions (mouse clicks, dialog boxes, track creation), rather than terminal windows editing XML/`.ardour` files.

integrity.description: The agent actually imported audio files rather than creating empty regions.
integrity.visual_evidence: The regions on the 'Guide Track' and 'Beep Track' display visible waveforms, indicating actual audio data was imported.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor to manually write the session configuration.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, or gedit) are shown editing the Ardour session files directly to bypass GUI interaction.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Ardour session must contain exactly three tracks named 'Guide Track', 'ADR Record', and 'Beep Track'. The loop range must be set from exactly 5.0 seconds to 15.0 seconds. The 'Beep Track' must contain exactly 3 distinct audio regions.
